name: overnight_with_adaptive_setback_and_wakeup
description: Warm summer day: afternoon cooling (14:00–22:00), bedtime at 22:30 applies P3 sleep band (ceiling=DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL=78F), morning wakeup at 07:00 restores comfort. Full overnight cycle.
notes: P3 migration (Issue #249): bedtime no longer uses comfort_cool + DEFAULT_SETBACK_DEPTH_COOL_F.
P3 uses select_comfort_band(in_sleep_window=True): ceiling = sleep_cool or DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL = 78F.
DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL = 78.0 (const.py). With comfort_cool=74, this is 4F above comfort ceiling.
No thermal model configured — uses default sleep band path.
NOTE: expect_temp updated from 77F (old formula) to 78F (DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL in P3).

events.note: Afternoon classification — cool mode starts at comfort_cool=74F
Evening: indoor cooling down, outdoor still warm — AC running normally
Bedtime: cool setback fires. Default depth = comfort_cool(74) + 3F = 77F. Ceiling=79F so 77F applies.
Overnight: indoor 76.5F with setback target 77F — AC barely running

assertions.reason: Afternoon classification: warm day, comfort band armed.
P3: comfort band ceiling=comfort_cool=74F confirms cool defense target at classification time.
P3 bedtime: sleep band ceiling = DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL = 78F (active=ceiling, warm night). Previously 77F = comfort_cool+3; P3 uses sleep_cool/DEFAULT_SLEEP_COOL directly.

verdict.summary: P3 overnight cycle: cool classification → sleep band (78F ceiling) → morning comfort restore (74F)
verdict.observed_behavior: classification_applied (74F) at 14:00; setback_applied (band ceiling=78F) at 22:30; comfort_restored (74F) at 07:00
verdict.expected_behavior: P3: sleep band [66/78] active=ceiling. Occupant sleeps in home set to 78F ceiling — cooler than daytime comfort 74F? Wait, 78>74. Sleepers tolerate warmer. Band floor 66F guards against overcooling during the night.
